messages.content: True or false? Canned messages are a quick and easy way to reach a wide audience in social media to find the right prospects.

False. Canned messages do not personalize the interaction with potential prospects and may come across as impersonal or spammy, which can turn people off from engaging with the brand. Personalized messages tailored to the target audience are more effective for establishing connections and building relationships.

How much of the buyer’s journey is digital?

20%

52%

90%

67%

When should a salesperson put forth the sales pitch in social media?

After sufficient rapport has been built and the customer is looking for what you have to sell

After a prospect follows them back on a social channel

After the first casual conversation you have with the person in social media

A month after you follow the prospect on social media

After sufficient rapport has been built and the customer is looking for what you have to sell. The sales pitch should come after establishing a relationship with the customer, which includes understanding their needs and pain points. Waiting until the customer is actively seeking what you have to offer increases the chances of success and avoids coming across as pushy or aggressive.
True or false? The world has changed and so has how we think about inbound, which is why advertising should be considered part of your marketing playbook.

True. The world is constantly changing and so are the preferences of the target audience, which is why it's important to keep up with the latest marketing trends and adapt accordingly. Inbound marketing alone may not be enough to reach all potential customers. Including advertising as part of the marketing strategy can help broaden the reach and attract new audiences through various channels.
Fill in the blank: “Facebook Ads is great for targeting based on _______, while Google Ads targets based on your audience’s ________.
Facebook Ads is great for targeting based on interests and demographics, while Google Ads targets based on your audience's search intent and behavior.
True or false? It’s always better to target your ads towards as broad a group of people as possible.

False. It is not always better to target ads towards as broad a group of people as possible. Targeting a specific audience that is more likely to be interested in the product or service being advertised is more effective than targeting a broad group of people who may not be interested. It saves resources and ensures that the advertising efforts are directed towards the right people, maximizing the chances of conversion and achieving desired results.

Which of the following would not contribute to ad fatigue?

Using the same image on your ad for over a month

Using only Facebook for your advertising strategy

Offering the same ebook in your ad for two months straight

All of these would contribute to ad fatigue

Using only Facebook for your advertising strategy would not necessarily contribute to ad fatigue, as long as the ads themselves are varied and not consistently repetitive. However, using the same image on your ad for over a month, offering the same ebook in your ad for two months straight, and generally repeating the same ad content frequently would all contribute to ad fatigue.

An ad for your upcoming webinar on network security should lead people directly to ___________.

a blog post on network security

your home page

a landing page where they can quickly sign up for the webinar

your About Us page

A landing page where they can quickly sign up for the webinar. The purpose of the ad is to promote the webinar and attract sign-ups, so directing people to a landing page specifically designed for that purpose is the most effective strategy. This allows visitors to quickly and easily provide their contact information and register for the webinar, increasing the chances of conversion and attendance.

What is one of the major benefits of Facebook Lead Ads?

Facebook will serve these ads on Pinterest in addition to Facebook.

They only serve your ads to a small group of highly qualified people.

The pre-filled form in Facebook makes it super easy for your audience to convert on—especially on mobile.

They’re optimized for a desktop audience, which is proven to convert better than mobile users.

The pre-filled form in Facebook makes it super easy for your audience to convert on—especially on mobile. The pre-filled form of Facebook Lead Ads allows users to quickly and easily fill in their information, such as name and email address, without ever having to leave Facebook. This convenience ensures that more people are likely to complete the lead form, which increases the chances of conversion. Additionally, since the ads are optimized for mobile, they can effectively reach users on-the-go and at any time, making them more accessible to a wider audience.

The number of leads you generate that actually become paying customers is known as your ________.

average conversion rate

average lead-to-customer rate

average revenue

average lifetime value of a customer

Average lead-to-customer rate. The number of leads that become paying customers is referred to as the lead-to-customer rate. This metric is an important component of the sales funnel that helps to measure the effectiveness of converting leads into actual customers. By tracking the lead-to-customer rate, you can gain insights into the strengths and weaknesses of your sales and marketing efforts and make necessary adjustments to optimize conversions.

Which one of the below choices is NOT a reason you should be measuring your social media ROI?

Prove the value of social media within your organization.

Show how social media can impact all departments, ranging beyond Marketing and Sales to HR to Engineering to PR.

Show influencers how enticing it would be to work with your company.

Understand and measure brand reputation and gain control of that conversation.

Show influencers how enticing it would be to work with your company. While measuring social media ROI can help with proving the value of social media, showing the impact of social media on various departments, understanding and measuring brand reputation, and gaining control of the conversation, it is not necessarily relevant or useful for showing influencers how enticing it would be to work with the company. The focus in demonstrating why influencers should collaborate with the company should be on other factors, such as the relevance of the company's products or services to the audience and the company's values and mission.

Which of the following social media expense do you want to track so you can calculate ROI on your marketing campaigns?

Work hours

Agency or freelance costs

Social media software and services

Content development expenses

All the above

All of the above. In order to calculate the ROI of social media marketing campaigns, it is important to track all expenses involved in the process. This includes tracking expenses such as work hours, agency or freelance costs, social media software and services, and content development expenses. By tracking these expenses, you can determine the total cost of the marketing campaign, which can then be compared to the revenue generated by the campaign to calculate its ROI.
True or false? When measuring your social media ROI, you calculate the effectiveness of an action by dividing the net income by the cost of this action and multiplying it by 100.

False. When measuring your social media ROI, you calculate the effectiveness of an action by dividing the net income by the cost of the action. This gives you a ratio or percentage which represents the return on investment. This percentage can then be used to determine the success of the social media campaign and identify areas for improvement. The formula is: (Net income / Cost of investment) x 100 = ROI.

Why do you need to use leading indicators of revenue success when calculating ROI on your social media strategy?

It’s difficult to know how much revenue is generated from a sale that originates from a social media campaign.

It can be months before you’ve closed new customers from a social media campaign.

Understanding how you stack up to your competition can help you pivot and make better business decisions.

Calculating ROI on social media is super hard to prove.

It can be months before you’ve closed new customers from a social media campaign. Leading indicators of revenue success are useful in calculating ROI on a social media strategy because it can take time for users to become customers after being introduced to a product or service through social media. These indicators can help businesses track engagement, website traffic, and other metrics that are likely to lead to conversions down the line. By tracking these leading indicators, businesses can more accurately predict future revenue success and adjust their social media strategy accordingly.
True or False? To implement an effective social media strategy, you have to be aware of the legal challenges, not ethical ones.

False. To implement an effective social media strategy, you have to be aware of both legal and ethical challenges. Social media users and companies are held accountable for their actions online, and it is essential to comply with legal requirements and ethical standards to avoid any negative consequences. Legal challenges include issues such as copyright infringement, data privacy, and defamation, while ethical challenges include concerns such as respecting customer privacy, being transparent, promoting truthful and accurate information, and avoiding offensive content. Addressing both legal and ethical challenges is important in building a strong online presence and maintaining a positive brand reputation.
A(n) ________ outlines guidelines and best practices for employees to follow on social media.

A social media policy outlines guidelines and best practices for employees to follow on social media. This policy provides clear direction on the appropriate use of social media by employees, including what kind of content is acceptable to share or post, how to respond to negative comments or feedback, and how to represent the company's brand accurately and professionally on social media. By establishing clear guidelines, businesses can ensure that employees understand their role in promoting the company's brand and avoiding any legal or ethical issues that may arise from social media use.

A(n) ________ outlines guidelines and best practices for employees to follow on social media.

crisis plan

advocacy plan

social media policy
Social media policy.

What’s the first step organizations and brands need to take when establishing a social media policy?

Educate employees on the importance of having a social media policy for all team members within the organization.

Reward the employees who are shining stars and are doing the right things on social media.

Research best case studies for brands that are using social media policies effectively.

Determine what not to do for social media policies for employees.

Educate employees on the importance of having a social media policy for all team members within the organization. The first step in establishing a social media policy is to ensure that all employees understand the need for such a policy and the risks involved in unmonitored social media use. By educating employees on the importance of the policy and its role in promoting the brand, businesses can ensure that they are using social media effectively in their roles and minimizing any potential risks. This education should also include examples of how the policy is intended to be applied in practical situations.

What is NOT an example of a social media crisis?

Employees going rogue on social media

A celebrity posting an update that sparks outrage

A hack into a Twitter account for a major brand

These are all examples of a social media crisis.
None of these are NOT an example of a social media crisis. They are all examples of a social media crisis.

The _________ stage focuses on creating message maps and listening protocols on social media.

preparation

recovery

response

crisis

Preparation. The preparation stage of crisis management focuses on preparing in advance by creating message maps and listening protocols on social media. By creating message maps, businesses can outline the key messages that need to be communicated to various stakeholders during a crisis. Listening protocols, on the other hand, outline how businesses should monitor and respond to conversations online in the event of a crisis. This stage is critical in ensuring that businesses are well-equipped to handle a crisis and respond effectively to any challenges that may arise.
True or false? Evaluating the team dynamics and how each member did in the crisis along with analyzing the reactions, KPIs, and overall response to the crisis happens during the response stage.

False. Evaluating the team dynamics and how each member did in the crisis along with analyzing the reactions, KPIs, and overall response to the crisis usually happens during the recovery stage of crisis management. The recovery stage involves assessing the effectiveness of the response to the crisis and learning from any mistakes or successes. This stage starts once the crisis is contained, and the immediate situation is resolved. At this point, businesses can analyze the performance of their team, assess the impact of the crisis on their KPIs and brand reputation, and make necessary changes or updates to their crisis management plan.
True or false: Ephemeral stories are quick bits of content, both video and photo, often with fun filters and “stickers” that disappear after a short period of time (1-24 hours).

True. Ephemeral stories are quick bits of content, both video and photo, often with fun filters and "stickers" that disappear after a short period of time (1-24 hours). The popularity of ephemeral stories, which are commonly found on social media platforms like Instagram, Snapchat, and Facebook, has grown rapidly in recent years as they provide an easy and engaging way for users to share and consume content.
